attributes.description: Alerts when a broker's memory utilization sustains above 85% (`activemq.memory.utilization > 0.85`). At this level producers are likely to be blocked and the broker is one incident away from refusing writes.
attributes.name: [ActiveMQ OTel] Broker memory utilization high

investigation_guide.blob:
## ActiveMQ broker memory utilization high

### What fired
`activemq.memory.utilization` averaged above 0.85 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
ActiveMQ reserves a bounded pool of JVM heap for buffering in-flight messages. As the pool fills, the broker applies back-pressure — first by slowing producers, then by blocking sends outright. Approaching 100% puts the broker in a near-unavailable state.

### Triage
1. Overview dashboard: confirm which broker(s) are affected.
2. Broker Health dashboard: inspect `activemq.memory.usage` vs `activemq.memory.limit` to see absolute pressure.
3. Correlate with `activemq.queue.blocked.sends` — if > 0 the broker is already blocking producers.
4. Check consumer counts on busy destinations; consumer starvation is the usual root cause.
5. Review JVM heap (`activemq.jvm.memory.heap.used` vs `.max`) — a broker under GC pressure drives this metric too.

### Remediation
- Unblock consumers / add consumer capacity for the backed-up destinations.
- Raise the broker's memory limit (`systemUsage/memoryUsage/limit`) if the workload has legitimately grown.
- Investigate message producers bursting into topics/queues without matching consumption capacity.

### Tuning
- `> 0.85` with a 15-minute window balances responsiveness and noise. Tighten to `> 0.80` for latency-sensitive tiers; loosen to `> 0.90` if your workload sustains high utilization in steady state.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-activemq.otel-*
// Broker memory utilization is a gauge (0–1 ratio)
| WHERE activemq.memory.utilization IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_util = MAX(AVG_OVER_TIME(activemq.memory.utilization)) BY activemq.broker.name, host.name
// Alert when memory utilization exceeds 85%; producers may be blocked
| WHERE max_util > 0.85
| SORT max_util DESC
| LIMIT 10
